methods.description: Process for testing the flexural and tensile strength of materials using standardized methods and equipment.

steps.description: Materials were printed into type 1B dumbbell specimens in accordance with EN ISO 527:2012 and EN ISO 178:2006.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare specimens according to EN ISO 527:2012 and EN ISO 178:2006 standards

steps.description: Tests of the specimens obtained were performed on a universal testing machine INSTRON 5969 with a maximum load force capacity of 50 kN.
steps.searchable_summary: perform mechanical tests using INSTRON 5969 machine

steps.description: The traverse speed for tensile strength measurements was set at 2 mm/min, and for the flexural strength, it was also set at 2 mm/min.
steps.searchable_summary: set traverse speed to 2 mm/min for tensile and flexural strength tests

methods.description: Procedure for performing Charpy impact tests on materials to assess their impact resistance.

steps.description: Charpy impact test (with no notch) was performed on an Instron Ceast 9050 impact machine according to ISO 179-1.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Charpy impact test using Instron Ceast 9050 according to ISO 179-1

methods.description: Method for determining the density of composites using the hydrostatic method.

steps.description: The density of the composites obtained was determined with the hydrostatic method using a Sartorius YCP04MS analytical balance at 20°C with the use of distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: measure composite density using hydrostatic method with Sartorius YCP04MS

methods.description: Procedure for observing the morphology and microstructure of composites using scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: Samples in the form of powders and moulds were dusted with cupro-nickel using a GATAN duster.
steps.searchable_summary: dust samples with cupro-nickel using GATAN duster

steps.description: Observations were conducted with a Hitachi SU 8000 scanning microscope with an accelerating voltage of 5 kV to image the preparations.
steps.searchable_summary: observe samples using Hitachi SU 8000 scanning microscope at 5 kV

methods.description: Analysis of surface structure and breakthroughs using a digital light microscope.

steps.description: Surface structure and breakthroughs were analysed under a Digital Light Microscope Keyence VHX 7000 with 100× to 1000× VH-Z100T lens.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze surface structure using Keyence VHX 7000 microscope

steps.description: All pictures were recorded with a VHX 7020 camera.

methods.description: Procedure for performing differential scanning calorimetry to analyze thermal properties of samples.

steps.description: Samples of 6 ± 0.2 mg were cut from each granulate and placed in an aluminium crucible with a punctured lid.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for DSC by cutting and placing in crucible

steps.description: The measurements were performed under nitrogen in the temperature range of 20-200°C and at a 10°C/min heating rate, and Tg was measured from the second heating cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DSC measurements under nitrogen from 20-200°C at 10°C/min
